title: Charza Kwinn
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image= |name=Charza Kwinn |homeworld=[[Pria/Legends|Pria]] |birth=Before [[129 BBY]]In ''[[Rogue Planet]]'', [[Thracia Cho Leem]] mentions that Kwinn has served the Jedi Order for "over a century". |death= |species=[[Priapulin]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height=4 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] (fully extended) |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] |masters= |apprentices= }} '''Charza Kwinn''' was a legendary [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Priapulin]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who was in the employ of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] for well over a [[Standard century/Legends|century]]. ==Biography== {{Dialogue|Kwinn|There are no pirates here, no commerce centers—indeed, the only inhabited planet for many light-years is Zonama Sekot. But Zonama Sekot is very close to the edge of the galaxy. Beyond this point, there is much that is not known. Anything could happen.|Skywalker|The edge of the galaxy! We could be the first beings to go beyond the edge! If we wanted to.|Kenobi|There are still frontiers, and that is a comforting thought.|Kwinn|Why comforting? Empty places without friends are not good!|Kenobi|The unknown is a place where we can discover who we truly are. So Qui-Gon taught me.|attr=Charza Kwinn, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]|src=''[[Rogue Planet]]''}} A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Priapulin]], Charza Kwinn was a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. By [[129 BBY]], he had become an ally of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], and was known to have transported needy [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to various [[Planet/Legends|planets]] and other locations in need of their services. In [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]], Kwinn transported the rogue [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Vergere]] aboard his [[YT-1150 cargo transport|YT-1150]] named the ''[[Star Sea Flower]]'' to the living planet [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]]; Kwinn left the world once ordered to, having received no orders to return for Vergere. He did note six ships in orbit as he left, and assumed them to be [[Sekotan Personal Starship]]s. A [[29 BBY/Legends|year later]], Kwinn transported two more Jedi, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], to Zonama Sekot. After dropping them off near the planet's north pole, he stayed in orbit and out of contact with the pair of Jedi. Soon, however, the planet was attacked by [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] of the [[Outland Regions Security Force/Legends|Republic Outland Regions Security Force]], who wanted the planet's abilities for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Kwinn participated in [[Second Battle of Zonama Sekot|the battle]], defending Zenoma Sekot and taking on a [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[Light cruiser/Legends|light cruiser]]. In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], following the establishment of [[High Human Culture/Legends|High Human Culture]] under the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Kwinn was forced to become a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] to support himself and his [[Crast|food-kin]], which he ate as well as provided for. Flying the ship the ''[[Coral Fin]]'', he limited his prey to Imperial vessels. His legacy inspired his people to assist the persecuted [[New Jedi Order]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|"Chazra comes from a species devoted to peace. He regards overt conflict as the grossest breach, and would rather die than fight. Still, he is intensely intelligent and extremely ambitious."
"So he makes a great spy?''"
"''A great spymaster. And an extraordinarily resourceful pilot."|[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] on Charza Kwinn|}} Kwinn was regarded as highly intelligent by the Jedi he transported, as well as having a sense of humor not easily understood by humans. He was a pacifist by nature, but that didn't stop him from enjoying aggressive competition among other species, finding it amusing. He had a symbiotic relationship with the [[Crast]], whom he referred to as food-kin. [[Priapulin|His kind]] used the species as food, and in return incubated and cared for their young. In addition to the normal Priapulin relationship with Crast, he also provided food and a place to live for his food-kin on the ''Star Sea Flower'', in return for them being the ship's crew. He was also a pilot of exceptional skill, having the ability to even outfly [[Sky mine]]s, in addition to [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|Droid Starfighters]]. He attributed part of his flying prowess to the ''Sea Star Flower'', whom he called his friend.
==Behind the scenes== Kwinn was introduced in the novel ''[[Rogue Planet]]'' by [[Greg Bear]]. He was subsequently mentioned in several reference books. Under the "''Coral Fin''" entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', he is incorrectly described as a Jedi Knight.{{CSWECite|I|139|''Coral Fin''}} ==Appearances== *''[[Rogue Planet]]'' {{1st|in book}} ==Sources== *[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']] *{{GamerCite|8|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Priapulin}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Charza Kwinn |fi=Charza Kwinn }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kwinn, Charza}} [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pilots]] [[Category:Pirates]] [[Category:Priapulins]] [[Category:Smugglers]]
